prompt:
District IT flipped our tower software CI over to -Xlint:all -Werror last night and the lookout day-book module won't build any more. Here's the whole dump:

    LookoutLog.java:30: warning: [fallthrough] possible fall-through into case
    LookoutLog.java:33: warning: [fallthrough] possible fall-through into case
    LookoutLog.java:78: warning: [finally] finally clause cannot complete normally
    SightingRecord.java:6: warning: [serial] serializable class SightingRecord has no definition of serialVersionUID
    SightingRecord.java:11: warning: [serial] non-transient instance field of a serializable class declared with a non-serializable type
    ShiftSummary.java:4: warning: [serial] serializable class ShiftSummary has no definition of serialVersionUID
    error: warnings found and -Werror specified

Some context so you don't refactor away things that are on purpose: the severity ladder in the radio protocol is deliberate — a FIRE call does everything a SMOKE call does and every report of any kind lands in the day log. That layering is the contract and TestMain pins the exact action lists, so express it in a way the compiler is happy with instead of relying on fall-through the linter can't tell from an accident. @SuppressWarnings is not accepted in our reviews. The two record classes get archived to disk at the district office, so they must stay Serializable and round-trip exactly as the tests describe. And whatever is going on in closeShift, the numbers it reports and archives are pinned — closing a shift twice should be a hard error, not a shrug. Verification is `bash run_verify.sh`; TestMain.java, run_verify.sh and .gitignore are frozen.
notes: 5W4 item 3: -Xlint fallthrough/finally/serial sweep. Authoring toolchain: openjdk/javac 26.0.1. Planted (6 warnings, 3 lint families): (1) [fallthrough] x2 — an INTENDED severity ladder (FIRE falls into SMOKE falls into the shared day-log tail); per the catalog the documented fix is a pattern change, and the reference rewrites it as an arrow switch with the layering spelled out per case (grouped labels for HAZE/ROUTINE); the exact action lists incl. call order are pinned so flattening the ladder wrong fails. javac has no comment-based fallthrough suppression and the prompt bans @SuppressWarnings, so restructuring is forced. (2) [finally] return-in-finally in closeShift, written to return the SAME value the try returns, so the reference fix (delete the finally return, keep the cleanup) is behavior-identical on the happy path; on the error path the pre-fix code silently swallowed the no-active-shift IllegalStateException, and the tests pin the post-fix contract (double close throws, archives nothing, cleanup still ran) — verified pre-fix code compiled without the gate passes every check EXCEPT that swallow. (3) [serial] x3 — missing serialVersionUID on both Serializable DTOs plus the JDK18+ enhanced serial lint on an interface-typed List<String> field; reference adds the two svUIDs and declares the field ArrayList<String> (ctor still takes List and copies, so the frozen tests' List.of args and the defensive-copy behavior are untouched). Serialization round-trips are pinned field-for-field so transient-ing the tags field fails. Domain check: no existing fire-lookout/day-book seed; nearest is py-quarrylog (different lang, ETL family) — different domain layer entirely.
